context: Checkpoint 2026-04-29 after post-compaction continuation. User clarified we are not architecturally limited to WASM targets; we should stay free/target-agnostic where useful while still leveraging WASM for plugin isolation/packaging. Streaming lane remains host-proxied: Tractor/native owns credentials, route policy, validation, transport internals; pi-agent/WASM consumes the bridge semantics. Real provider stream:true remains disabled until end-to-end partial persistence is proven.
current_tasks: Current unpushed stack since origin/develop now includes latest commits: 6b255008 docs(agent): generalize streaming readiness comment; eba6ff40 docs(agent): note native stream parser seam; a422b2d5 feat(tractor): draft stream chunk sequencing; a2b19432 feat(tractor): parse llm stream text deltas; 0d3e5605 feat(tractor): add target-neutral sse event parser; e98cf70a docs(agent): clarify streaming target strategy; 3a3d59e8 refactor(agent): route stream-ready requests to host bridge; 63f18c0b fix(tractor): validate stream bridge metadata; plus prior stream bridge commits back to 2b91ffe1.

ADR-053 updated: streaming core should be target-agnostic where practical. Native Tractor owns transport/buffering/validation/persistence boundaries; WASM is the plugin boundary, not a forced architectural constraint.

Tractor stream bridge metadata validation added in packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge/core.rs. It blocks blank/unsafe prompt-ref/model/provider-family before even using buffered placeholder path. Tests added to packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge_tests/llm_stream_contract.rs.

pi-agent request path now routes to http_post_stream_via_host only when provider_stream_request_enabled_from_env() is true and active stream metadata exists. Because streaming_reader_available() is still false, this is a future-ready seam only and current runtime behavior remains buffered.

New target-neutral Tractor stream internals in packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge/llm_stream_events.rs: parse_sse_data_events, parse_stream_text_deltas_from_sse, parse_stream_text_deltas, stream_text_chunk_drafts_from_sse, last_stream_text_chunk_sequence. Tests cover SSE comments/DONE/CRLF/multiline/trailing events, OpenAI deltas, Anthropic content_block_delta, invalid JSON ignore, and sequence assignment.
ROADMAP updated: host WIT contract done, pi-agent wrapper seam done, native Tractor SSE/delta/sequence draft helpers done. Chunked HTTP/SSE delivery through complete-http-stream remains pending.
streaming_config comments updated away from WASM-only wording: readiness is now host-proxied streaming transport readiness.
blockers: Real streaming is still disabled. streaming_reader_available() remains false; provider stream:true is not emitted even with LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES=1.
complete-http-stream still uses buffered compatibility placeholder. It validates metadata and returns final_body/last_sequence/stored_chunks=0, but does not incrementally read or persist chunks.

Tractor now has provider delta parsing duplicated conceptually from pi-agent. Before deepening, decide whether to extract a shared crate/module or accept host-owned parser duplication for the first native transport slice.
Disk after clean:light: ~8.1GB free; Rust targets ~4.86GB. Avoid broad/global checks.
next_actions: Next slice: design/implement live complete-http-stream internals behind tests using a fake SSE server. Keep it native/target-neutral in Tractor; do not flip streaming_reader_available until partial persistence is proven.

Decide storage shape for host-streamed partials: direct AgentResponse projection, generic LlmStreamChunk observation, or both. Given user guidance, do not let WASM-only concerns dominate; favor native host extensibility plus WASM compatibility.

If implementing direct persistence, add pure JSON node builders/tests first, then wire NativeSync store calls. Ensure sequences returned by complete-http-stream match the last successfully stored chunk.
If pushing: local checks passed after latest stack. Use git push origin develop and gh run watch --exit-status for the resulting run. Do not manually poll CI.
At start of next session: git status --short; git log --oneline origin/develop..HEAD; df -h /workspaces/refarm; npm run clean:rust:check --silent.
open_questions: Should host store AgentResponse nodes directly for immediate WebSocket compatibility, generic LlmStreamChunk nodes for extensibility, or both?
Should the OpenAI/Anthropic SSE delta parser move into a shared target-neutral crate used by pi-agent and Tractor?
What exact persistence semantics are desired if the stream errors after storing some partial chunks?
Should complete-http-stream return final provider JSON bytes only, or also include a compact host-side stream summary/error status?
When to flip streaming_reader_available() true: after fake SSE host tests only, or after full pi-agent harness proves partial AgentResponse nodes visible to clients?
key_decisions_pending: Host partial storage shape: AgentResponse vs generic chunk vs dual-write.
Shared parser extraction vs host parser ownership.
Live transport implementation and test harness shape.
Error/cancel semantics for partially persisted streams.
When to push the current unpushed stack.
